metadata.title: States PCS: Gujarat GK Questions for GPSC Exams

questions.question: Jesore Hills, part of the Aravalli mountain range, are located between which two places in Gujarat?
questions.explanation: Jesore Hills lie between Danta and Palanpur in Gujarat, forming part of the Aravalli mountain range.

questions.question: What is the name of the safari park near the Statue of Unity in Gujarat where giraffes are kept in captivity?
questions.explanation: The tallest animal found in Gujarat is the giraffe, present in captivity at the safari park near the Statue of Unity in Kevadia. Giraffes are not native to India.

questions.question: Which place in Gujarat is renowned for the Salt March led by Mahatma Gandhi in 1930?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi broke the unjust salt law during the Dandi March in 1930, starting on March 12 and culminating on April 6, protesting British salt monopoly.

questions.question: In which village in Gandhinagar district, Gujarat, is India’s most modern Mother Dairy facility located?
questions.explanation: Gandhinagar district in Gujarat houses India’s most modern Mother Dairy facility, located in Bhat village, equipped with advanced technology for dairy processing and packaging.

questions.question: Who was appointed as the Mahamatya (Prime Minister) of Gujarat by King Vishaladeva Vaghela in the early 13th century?
questions.explanation: Vastupala was appointed Mahamatya (Prime Minister) of Gujarat by King Vishaladeva Vaghela and his predecessor Viradhavala in the early 13th century.

questions.question: How many major dams are there in Gujarat?
questions.explanation: Gujarat has 14 major dams, 193 medium dams, and 595 minor dams, essential for water storage, irrigation, and hydroelectric power generation.

questions.question: Which city is the most populous in the Indian state of Gujarat?
questions.explanation: Ahmedabad is the most populous city in Gujarat, known as a major economic and industrial hub with significant cultural and commercial importance.

questions.question: Who was the founder of the Solanki dynasty in Gujarat?
questions.explanation: The Solanki dynasty, significant in early medieval Gujarat history, was founded by Mulraj Solanki, marking an era noted for architectural and cultural advancements.

questions.question: In which fort are the ancient Uparkot Caves, dating back to the 2nd–3rd century BCE, located in Gujarat?
questions.explanation: Uparkot Caves in Junagadh’s Uperkot Fort are ancient rock-cut Buddhist caves from the 2nd–3rd century BCE, part of a group including Khapra Kodiya and Baba Pyare caves.

questions.question: In which city of Gujarat is the Institute of Plasma Research (IPR) located?
questions.explanation: The Institute of Plasma Research (IPR) is located in Gandhinagar, Gujarat, near the Sabarmati River between Ahmedabad and Gandhinagar.
